title: vitis vitiginea var. campestris
intro: Vitis vitiginea var. campestris, also known as wild grapevine or forest grape, is a plant species within the Vitaceae family that is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its leaves and bark, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits associated with Vitis vitiginea var. campestris include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as its use as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
